response.justification: The candidate is a versatile senior engineer with genuine geospatial domain exposure (founder of GISCollective, Green Map GIS work) and proven low-latency systems experience in DLang at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, plus some C/C++ at National Instruments. However, the role's core requirement — modern C++ or Rust in a commercial setting — is weakly evidenced and dated (C++ work ended in 2015), recent years are dominated by TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, and there is no direct experience with routing engines, tiling pipelines, or navigation guidance systems. Worth a conversation only if systems fundamentals prove transferable.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data platform experience (GISCollective, Green Map, FOSS4G talk)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (millions of req/sec, async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang)
Strong engineering craft: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, Linux and Kubernetes operations
concerns: Modern C++/Rust commercial experience is minimal and roughly a decade old
No direct routing, map-tiling, or navigation guidance pipeline experience
Recent focus is TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, far from the role's systems core
